title: Project Report: CodeQuest Admin Power Platform Suite — Microsoft Power Platform Developer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Model-driven app + embedded Power BI + ALM across environments for school admin reporting.
objectives: Embedded Power BI class performance tile
Security roles for admin and teacher
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Advanced)*

**CodeQuest Admin Power Platform Suite** is a advanced-level end-to-end project for Microsoft Power Platform Developer. Duration: 4–5 weeks. Model-driven app + embedded Power BI + ALM across environments for school admin reporting. Tools: Power Apps, Power BI, Dataverse, Solutions.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Model-driven app for student/report management
Objective: Embedded Power BI class performance tile
Objective: Solution ALM dev → test → prod
Objective: Security roles for admin and teacher
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Microsoft Power Platform Developer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Microsoft Power Platform Developer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Microsoft Power Platform Developer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Model App
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Model App** *(Level: Advanced)*

Dataverse tables. Complete these tasks: Students; Reports; Marks. Deliverable for this phase: Model app published.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Students
- [ ] Reports
- [ ] Marks
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Model App is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Microsoft Power Platform Developer agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Model app published to the teacher.

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Advanced)*

Submit all final deliverables: Published app; BI dashboard embed; ALM doc; Security matrix. Skills demonstrated: Model-driven apps, Power BI, ALM. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Published app
Deliverable: BI dashboard embed
Deliverable: ALM doc
Deliverable: Security matrix
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Published app and passes the rubric.
key_takeaways: Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.
